audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:clause + of + clause]] and [[grammar:deze/dit + noun]]. Use [[grammar:of]] between two choices. Use [[grammar:deze]] with a [[grammar:de-word]] and [[grammar:dit]] with a [[grammar:het-word]].
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:clause + of + clause]]: [[grammar:Of]] coordinates alternatives and can also introduce an embedded polar question in later chapters.; [[grammar:deze/dit + noun]]: The proximal demonstrative agrees with the noun’s grammatical gender class.

audienceSections.normal:
#### clause + of + clause

Use [[grammar:of]] between two choices. Example: [[grammar:Nemen we tomaten of komkommers?]]

#### deze/dit + noun

Use [[grammar:deze]] with a [[grammar:de-word]] and [[grammar:dit]] with a [[grammar:het-word]]. Example: [[grammar:Deze tomaat is rijp, maar deze komkommer is hard.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### clause + of + clause

[[grammar:Of]] coordinates alternatives and can also introduce an embedded polar question in later chapters. Literal example: [[grammar:Nemen we tomaten of komkommers?]]

#### deze/dit + noun

The proximal demonstrative agrees with the noun’s grammatical gender class. Literal example: [[grammar:Deze tomaat is rijp, maar deze komkommer is hard.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Of]] links alternatives. [[grammar:Deze]] goes with [[grammar:de-words]]; [[grammar:dit]] goes with [[grammar:het-words]]. Here, alleen means only, not by oneself as in Chapter 18.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Of]] links alternatives. [[grammar:Deze]] goes with [[grammar:de-words]]; [[grammar:dit]] goes with [[grammar:het-words]]. Here, alleen has the restrictive meaning only, distinct from the Chapter 18 meaning alone. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Bij deze kraam is de groente vers.]] — At this stall the vegetables are fresh.
- [[grammar:Nemen we tomaten of komkommers?]] — Shall we take tomatoes or cucumbers?
- [[grammar:Deze tomaat is rijp, maar deze komkommer is hard.]] — This tomato is ripe, but this cucumber is hard.
- [[grammar:Wil je dit mandje gebruiken?]] — Do you want to use this basket?
- [[grammar:Ja, dit mandje is licht.]] — Yes, this basket is light.
- [[grammar:Dan kies ik de tomaten voor de soep.]] — Then I choose the tomatoes for the soup.
- [[grammar:Ik neem ook deze komkommer; hij ruikt fris.]] — I will also take this cucumber; it smells fresh.
- [[grammar:Kiezen we ook fruit of alleen groente?]] — Are we also choosing fruit or only vegetables?
- [[grammar:Alleen groente; thuis hebben we appels.]] — Only vegetables; at home we have apples.
- [[grammar:Sophie neemt de tomaten en Noor draagt het mandje.]] — Sophie takes the tomatoes and Noor carries the basket.
- [[grammar:Goed. Dan is onze keuze klaar.]] — Good. Then our choice is settled.
- [[grammar:En ik betaal bij de kraam.]] — And I will pay at the stall.
